data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Ă— THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57), repo-corroborated. ONE high-confidence finding; ordering/which-path-survives is RESOLVED by the declared depends_on edge.

RESOLVED (not filed): 2359 declares `depends_on 33d0` (confirmed in deps; "Sequencing: depends on story D â€¦ so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 collapses to ONE production path first; 2359 then instruments the single survivor â€” the same-region co-edit of batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py is sequenced, not concurrent. Ordering + implicit-shared-state read benign.

FINDING â€” conflicting assumptions: `labels_applied` is list-valued in the existing/surviving payload, but 2359's count-based `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary assumes a uniform int; the surviving-applier label representation is a contract shared with 33d0 and stated by neither.
- finding: 2359 AC1 surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as `links_applied`/`comments_applied`, and 2359 AC2/AC5 define a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-invariant canary "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied." Repo-confirmed, the per-sub-op signals are NOT uniformly int: `labels_applied` is `list[str]` (apply_outbound.py L245/L257/L260/L389) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` (L325/L278) and inbound `links_applied` (apply_inbound.py L469) are ints. The `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list (`[]` â‰  `0`; a non-empty list is truthy â€” note the existing no-op guard at apply_outbound.py L372-378 deliberately uses truthiness `not labels_applied`, NOT `== 0`). Flipping `labels_applied` to an int is itself a contract change to a key the existing tests assert by exact list contents. Because 33d0 ("Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path") owns the surviving applier whose payload these tests assert, the label representation is shared between 2359 (adds the canary) and 33d0 (owns the survivor's payload) â€” neither states the canonical representation, so the canary's well-definedness for labels is unowned even with the depends_on edge.
  - criteria: 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied), 2359 AC2 + AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 canary); parent AC "sub-op telemetry surfaced" + parent AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly"; vs 33d0 AC1/AC2 (surviving-applier payload contract)
  - location: 2359 What/AC1/AC2/AC5; apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (append `+`/`-` label strings), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L325/L278 (int), L372-378 no-op guard (truthiness, not `== 0`); apply_inbound.py L469 (inbound `links_applied: int` â€” cited parity model); tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233/L296/L327 (exact-list asserts on labels_applied â€” the tests 33d0 retargets)
  - evidence: repo search confirms labels_applied is list[str] while links/comments_applied and inbound links_applied are ints; existing tests assert exact label-list contents (not a count); the production batch path carries no per-sub-op counter today, so 33d0's parity gate must mint the label representation, on which 2359 then layers a count canary
  - scenarios: (a) survivor keeps labels_applied as a list â†’ 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires/never-fires (`[]` â‰  `0`; non-empty list truthy) â†’ silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op while links/comments are covered; (b) labels_applied flipped to int â†’ exact-list asserts at L204/L233/L296/L327 (retargeted by 33d0) break â†’ parent "suite green" / 2359 AC4 "sync semantics otherwise unchanged" regresses; (c) representation left unstated â†’ 33d0's payload contract and 2359's canary disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads
  - impact: 2359 AC2's count-invariant canary is not well-defined as stated for labels without a representation decision; risk of breaking an existing asserted payload key (parent "suite green"); parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" may hold for links/comments but NOT labels
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes â€” conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload label representation between 2359's count-canary and 33d0's surviving applier)
  - suggested_fix: in 2359, specify the label normalization explicitly â€” canary uses `len(labels_applied)` (or a derived `labels_applied_count`) while leaving the list-valued `labels_applied` key intact so exact-list tests survive â€” and coordinate with 33d0 so the surviving applier exposes the label signal in ONE representation across the single production path; state whether `computed`/`applied` for labels is counted over the labels-list length so the predicate is well-typed.

BENIGN (not filed): implicit shared state / ordering â€” sequenced via depends_on 33d0; dependency gap â€” only input dep (33d0) correctly declared, no schema/fixture input needed from 8937/fe57; scope overlap â€” 2359 canary vs 33d0 AC2 combined UPDATE vs 822a AC1 bidirectional round-trip are distinct intents over the same production path, with consolidation/probe-wiring declared OUT; consumer impact â€” added RECON sub-op fields are additive, AC5 asserts the count invariant not log strings; residual references â€” none, warn-first flag + counters are additive plumbing on the surviving path. Parent anti-change-detector-guardrail is a G3 coverage question owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause â€” not a G4 matter.
